productLevelDefinition.label: LV3 immersive advisor system
productLevelDefinition.note: In this automation, LV3 means architecture, experience, interface, and minimal onboarding maturity for the advisor workflow. It does not by itself mean Level 3 public launch readiness unless the release hardening docs and operator approvals say so.
targetExperience: 建立可編輯關係圖，且每個人物可附職位、年薪、狀態、關係脈絡與待確認資訊
由客戶與關係圖生成拜訪準備包
準備包以 multi-step 推論產生報告、問題清單、推論依據、待確認事項與下一步
從準備包一鍵建立 AI 劇場舞台，舞台環境來自客戶關係圖與準備包
劇場支援私聊、群聊、焦點角色、人物狀態更新與關係張力調整
AI 訪談可建立或補強客戶資料、關係圖、準備包與劇場設定
整體介面維持現代、簡約、低噪音、容易上手，但不犧牲專業深度

priorityWorkstreams: PIM: Realtime Voice / Park-style Memory Interview
ITA: Interview x Theater Dual Agent
LCH: Launch Readiness Three AI / DB-backed Client CRUD
NAP: NANDA / AgentFacts protocol alignment and AI registry readiness
MM/AIS/RAS: Minimal UI, AI-first navigation, role-aware surfaces

candidateQueue.goal: Make client creation flow naturally continue into relationship graph editing with person metadata and compliance-safe confirmation boundaries.
Generate a visit preparation package from client facts, relationship graph, policies, interview memories, and explicit unknowns.
Show the preparation package question list with supporting evidence, inference labels, unknown gaps, and advisor-confirmation controls.
Create a theater stage directly from the preparation package and relationship graph, including roles, goals, tensions, and scene constraints.
Support group/private theater interaction and safe person-state updates that can change stage relationships without rewriting confirmed CRM facts.
Let AI interview create or enrich client profile, relationship graph, preparation package, and theater stage through confirmation/writeback cards.
Prove the entire flow is understandable from a clean browser context with low-noise UI, no raw-ID workflow, mobile safety, and API/browser evidence.

Make every ASAI AI module internally describable, capability-declared, least-disclosure, adapter-ready, versioned, and assigned a registry readiness state before any external NANDA or third-party registry publication.

cadenceReview.nextRecommendedImplementationSlice: NAP-003b interview memory + quick-capture/realtime source adoption completed on 2026-06-21: asai.interview.companion, asai.interview.quick_capture, and asai.interview.realtime_voice now carry proof.sourceAdoption.status=adopted with source owner refs, evidence refs, no-provider/usage notes, writeback confirmation boundaries, and realtime dry-run/event-mirror/transcription separation. Because normalLoopsSinceLastWholeProductReview is now 4, the next heartbeat should run the fifth-loop whole-product gap review prompt before resuming implementation; after review compare NAP-003c theater/RAG source adoption vs NAP-005 local-only adapter/export dry-run vs a clean LV3 cross-flow no-provider proof.
cadenceReview.policy: Run four narrow LV3 implementation/proof loops, then run one whole-product architecture, experience, interface, safety, and onboarding review loop before resuming implementation.

safetyBoundaries: Never remove or make optional client/policy complianceChecklist, sensitivityLevel, or kycStatus.
Never break the SPIN SITUATION -> PROBLEM -> IMPLICATION -> NEED_PAYOFF state machine.
Only ITA-003/ITA-006 may perform the approved Theater Route B migration, and it must include migration, rollback/compatibility note, QA, and AiUsageLog.
Every OpenAI/Anthropic provider call must write AiUsageLog or an explicit guarded-disabled/non-provider proof.
Do not store raw cookies, secrets, tokens, OTP, raw provider payloads, raw private transcripts, or raw payment data in reports or evidence.
Do not perform production write, real email, real notification, real payment/refund, destructive DB operation, or remote deletion without explicit approval.

alwaysRun: git status --short --branch at start and end
runWhenRelevant: pnpm exec prisma db push only when target and approval match AGENTS.md
targeted API/browser proof for the selected slice

gitPolicy.pushHoldReason: User requested: 先不用 git push. Automation loops should still validate, stage, and commit locally, then report push skipped by user instruction.
